{"parsed":{"_path":"/articles/ecommerce-giants-market-dominance","_dir":"articles","_draft":false,"_partial":false,"_locale":"","title":"E-Commerce Giants: How Amazon and Flipkart Reshaped Retail and Labor","description":"From disruption to dominance—how Amazon and Flipkart conquered retail markets, destroyed traditional merchants, and created a new underclass of gig workers.","author":"Staff","date":"2026-05-05","category":"Economics","tags":["ecommerce","amazon","flipkart","retail","labor"],"keywords":"amazon, flipkart, ecommerce, online shopping, retail disruption","body":{"type":"root","children":[{"type":"element","tag":"h2","props":{"id":"the-disruption-that-became-monopoly"},"children":[{"type":"text","value":"The Disruption That Became Monopoly"}]},{"type":"element","tag":"p","props":{},"children":[{"type":"text","value":"2010: Amazon had 26% of US ecommerce. convenience and lower prices relative to 2010 retail. But those gains are captured by wealthy, urban consumers with Prime memberships. Rural, lower-income consumers pay more and wait longer. The aggregate gain is real but unequally distributed."}]},{"type":"element","tag":"p","props":{},"children":[{"type":"element","tag":"strong","props":{},"children":[{"type":"text","value":"For merchants:"}]},{"type":"text","value":" You're trapped in a monopoly system. Amazon/Flipkart offer scale you can't access elsewhere, but extract 30-45% rent. Your best strategy: Build direct-to-consumer brand (Shopify, Instagram), use ecommerce platforms as supplement not anchor."}]},{"type":"element","tag":"p","props":{},"children":[{"type":"element","tag":"strong","props":{},"children":[{"type":"text","value":"For labor:"}]},{"type":"text","value":" Retail job destruction continues. Ecommerce offers fewer, worse jobs. If you were a retail employee in 2010, ecommerce likely made you worse off. If you're entering the job market in 2026, retail isn't an option—you're forced into gig work or other sectors."}]},{"type":"element","tag":"p","props":{},"children":[{"type":"element","tag":"strong","props":{},"children":[{"type":"text","value":"For policy:"}]},{"type":"text","value":" Ecommerce consolidation is complete. Amazon and Flipkart won. Antitrust enforcement is your only lever for creating competition. Without intervention, expect monopoly pricing to intensify."}]},{"type":"element","tag":"hr","props":{},"children":[]},{"type":"element","tag":"p","props":{},"children":[{"type":"element","tag":"em","props":{},"children":[{"type":"text","value":"E-commerce promised disruption. It delivered consolidation. Consumers and sellers benefited initially, then found themselves trapped in a system where two platforms extract enormous rents while destroying the employment structures that preceded them."}]}]}],"toc":{"title":"","searchDepth":2,"depth":2,"links":[{"id":"the-disruption-that-became-monopoly","depth":2,"text":"The Disruption That Became
